title_en: Maliki Taharah and Salah — Key Positions
content_en:
The Mudawwanah's treatment of taharah and salah reveals the distinctively Madinan character of the Maliki school, a character shaped by Imam Malik's famous doctrine that the practice of the people of Madinah constitutes a binding legal authority in its own right. This principle — 'amal ahl al-Madinah — appears repeatedly in Ibn al-Qasim's answers when he explains why Malik diverged from a hadith that other scholars accepted.

On wudu, the Maliki school holds several positions that distinguish it from the other three schools. The obligation of niyyah (intention) for wudu is affirmed, but the Malikis require that the niyyah be present at the very beginning of the act — specifically at the washing of the face, which is the first obligatory element. Renewing niyyah midway is insufficient if it was absent at the start. This reflects Malik's general emphasis on the inward dimension of worship being synchronized with its outward beginning.

The question of mash (wiping) over footwear is treated conservatively in the Maliki school. While Malik permitted wiping over leather socks (khuffayn), his school became known for restricting this permission compared to the Hanafi and Hanbali schools, particularly regarding the duration and conditions. The Mudawwanah records detailed discussions about what constitutes a valid khuff and when the permission lapses.

Perhaps most distinctive is the Maliki position on the basmala in salah. Malik held that 'Bismillah ir-Rahman ir-Rahim' should not be recited aloud at the beginning of al-Fatiha in congregational prayer, and that reciting it quietly is permitted but not required. This contrasts sharply with the Shafi'i position that the basmala is a verse of al-Fatiha and must be recited. Malik based his position on the practice he witnessed in Madinah, arguing that three generations of Muslims in the Prophet's own city had not recited it aloud.

On the qunoot supplication, Malik's school differs from others by restricting the standing du'a in Fajr prayer. The Mudawwanah records Malik's view that qunoot in Fajr is recommended, a position that contrasts with the Hanafi school's rejection of it and the Shafi'i position that it is sunnah mu'akkadah.

The Maliki school's treatment of congregational prayer reflects the Madinan tradition in other ways as well. The imam's recitation of Fatiha suffices for those praying behind him — the follower need not recite anything in the portions where the imam recites aloud. This position, shared with the Hanafis, rests on the hadith 'When the imam recites, be silent' and on the observed practice of the Madinan community.

The Mudawwanah also contains extensive discussion of the Friday prayer and its conditions. Malik required that the khutbah be delivered in Arabic, that it praise Allah and send salawat on the Prophet, and that it contain a genuine admonishment. The minimum congregation for Friday prayer was set at twelve free adult men according to one well-known Maliki position, though the Mudawwanah records some disagreement on this number among Maliki scholars.
title_id: Taharah dan Salat Maliki — Posisi-Posisi Kunci
content_id:
Pembahasan Al-Mudawwanah tentang taharah dan salat mengungkapkan karakter Madinah yang khas dari mazhab Maliki, sebuah karakter yang dibentuk oleh doktrin terkenal Imam Malik bahwa praktik penduduk Madinah merupakan otoritas hukum yang mengikat sejajar dengan hadis. Karena Madinah adalah kota tempat Nabi ﷺ hidup, mengajar, dan meninggal, Malik berpendapat bahwa cara masyarakat Madinah beribadah merupakan transmisi langsung dari sunnah yang hidup (sunnah 'amaliyyah).

Dalam taharah, ciri khas Maliki yang paling menonjol adalah pandangan bahwa air dalam jumlah besar (qullatayn ke atas) tidak menjadi najis kecuali berubah sifatnya, sementara untuk air dalam jumlah sedikit pun, najis yang tidak mengubah sifatnya dianggap tidak memengaruhi kesucian air dalam kondisi tertentu. Pendekatan pragmatis ini mencerminkan konteks lingkungan di mana banyak komunitas Muslim awal hidup — di daerah yang air bersihnya terbatas.

Posisi paling khas mazhab Maliki dalam salat adalah sadl (melepas tangan di samping tubuh saat berdiri dalam salat), yang bertentangan dengan posisi Hanafi dan Syafi'i yang meletakkan tangan di atas dada atau perut. Imam Malik berargumen bahwa inilah cara penduduk Madinah salat, dan ini merupakan transmisi langsung dari praktik Nabi ﷺ. Al-Mudawwanah merekam pembahasan ini dengan detail yang mencukupi untuk memahami landasan posisi Maliki.

Pembahasan tentang qunut dalam salat Subuh juga khas. Mazhab Maliki mewajibkan qunut di salat Subuh sebagai sunnah mu'akkadah, berdasarkan amal penduduk Madinah yang terus mempraktikkannya. Ini berbeda dari mazhab Hanafi yang tidak menganjurkan qunut Subuh dan mazhab Syafi'i yang merekomendasikannya. Al-Mudawwanah memberikan dasar pemikiran Maliki untuk posisi ini dengan merujuk pada praktik Madinah yang otoritatif.
